quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Covalent Bonding and Bond Polarity
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Covalent Bond Formation, Single/Double/Triple Bonds, Polar vs Nonpolar Covalent Bonds, Electronegativity and Bond Polarity

questions.question: A covalent bond is formed when atoms:
questions.explanation: A covalent bond is formed when atoms share one or more pairs of electrons. Ionic bonds involve electron transfer.

questions.question: Covalent bonding usually occurs between:
questions.explanation: Covalent bonding typically occurs between non-metals. Metals and non-metals usually form ionic bonds, and noble gases rarely form bonds at all.

questions.question: Which molecule contains a single covalent bond?
questions.explanation: H₂ contains a single covalent bond (one shared pair of electrons). O₂ has a double bond, N₂ has a triple bond, and CO₂ has two double bonds.

questions.question: Which of the following forms a covalent compound?
questions.explanation: H₂O (water) is a covalent compound formed by sharing electrons between hydrogen and oxygen. NaCl, MgO, and CaCl₂ are all ionic compounds.

questions.question: A double covalent bond involves sharing of:
questions.explanation: A double covalent bond involves sharing two pairs of electrons (four electrons total). For example, in O₂, each oxygen shares two pairs.

questions.question: Which molecule contains a double bond?
questions.explanation: O₂ contains a double bond (O=O). H₂ and Cl₂ have single bonds, and NaCl is ionic.

questions.question: A triple covalent bond consists of:
questions.explanation: A triple covalent bond consists of three shared pairs of electrons (six electrons total). For example, in N₂, each nitrogen shares three pairs.

questions.question: Which molecule contains a triple bond?
questions.explanation: N₂ (nitrogen gas) contains a triple bond (N≡N). O₂ has a double bond, CO₂ has double bonds, and H₂O has single bonds.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a covalent compound?
questions.explanation: NaCl (sodium chloride) is an ionic compound, not covalent. CO₂, NH₃, and CH₄ are all covalent compounds.

questions.question: In the molecule HCl, the bond is:
questions.explanation: HCl has a polar covalent bond because chlorine is more electronegative than hydrogen, pulling the shared electrons toward itself and creating partial charges (δ⁺ on H, δ⁻ on Cl).

questions.question: A polar covalent bond occurs when:
questions.explanation: A polar covalent bond occurs when electrons are unequally shared between atoms due to a difference in electronegativity. Equal sharing gives nonpolar covalent bonds, and ion formation gives ionic bonds.

questions.question: In covalent bonding, atoms share electrons to:
questions.explanation: Atoms share electrons in covalent bonding to achieve stability, typically by attaining a full valence shell (octet rule). This lowers the potential energy of the system and results in a more stable arrangement than separate atoms. Forming ions (A) is characteristic of ionic bonding, and sharing electrons does not increase charge (C) or electronegativity (D).

questions.question: Covalent compounds usually do NOT conduct electricity because:
options: They are composed of charged ions that are locked tightly within a solid crystal lattice.
They consist of neutral molecules and lack free-moving electrons or ions to carry a charge.
They possess extremely high melting and boiling points that prevent the flow of current.
They undergo a complete dissociation into mobile ions when dissolved in a polar solvent.
questions.explanation: Covalent compounds do not conduct electricity because they consist of neutral molecules with no free-moving charged particles (ions or electrons). Option A describes ionic compounds in solid state, option C is false (covalent compounds often have low melting points), and option D describes ionic compounds dissolving.

questions.question: Which factor determines bond polarity?
questions.explanation: Bond polarity is determined by the difference in electronegativity between the two atoms. A larger difference (0.5–1.7) gives a polar covalent bond.

questions.question: Which molecule has non-polar covalent bonds?
questions.explanation: O₂ has non-polar covalent bonds because both atoms are identical with the same electronegativity, so electrons are shared equally. HCl, NH₃, and H₂O all have polar covalent bonds.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered covalent bonding and bond polarity! 🧪⚡
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between single, double, and triple bonds.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on what makes a bond polar vs nonpolar.
scoreMessages.0: Please review covalent bond formation, bond types, and electronegativity's role in polarity.
